title: Gooey Midnight Polish Treacle Hash
description: A bitter, chewy, and indulgent Polish dish that's perfect for midnight cravings, made with treacle on a brine-wet low-calorie-friendly tava-griddle.
instructions: Heat the tava-griddle over medium heat.
Sprinkle a small amount of water to check the heat, the water should sizzle and evaporate quickly.
In a large bowl, mix together the treacle, 1/4 cup of low-calorie-friendly vinegar, and 1/4 cup of water.
Add 1 tablespoon of mustard seeds and mix well.
Add the mixture to the preheated tava-griddle and spread it evenly.
Let it cook for 5 minutes on the first side, until the edges start to set.
Flip the hash and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the second side is caramelized and sticky.
Let it cool for a minute before slicing and serving.
Slice the hash into small pieces and serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
